tags.notes: Geothermal vent site in SSMR; bubbles rise from sandy seabed creating champagne effect; fringing reef slopes to ~30m near-perpendicular wall section; shore entry with boardwalk
Swiss Cheese formation + Soldierfish Cave swim-through; pinnacle swim-through at 35ft leads to crater wall dropping 120ft+; SSMR rates this Moderate (2 flags), not Advanced
Three pinnacles rising from 25ft shelf near Soufriere village; easy second-dive option; site_type corrected from wall to pinnacle based on all sources
5 pinnacles on north edge of Soufriere crater; depth 40-80ft (12-24m); reef sharks confirmed (girlsthatscuba); winter schooling jacks activity; barrel sponges
Shallow colourful reef in Batali Bay; finger and pencil coral; frogfish various colors; garden eels; upside-down jellyfish on sand; occasional nurse sharks under ledges; entry can be shore or boat

1500ft wall; suitable for all levels per SSMR, Dive Dominica, and DAN — difficulty downgraded from Advanced to Intermediate; sandy shelf start at 25ft with very little current; black coral, whip corals, gorgonians confirmed
Tunnels at 25 and 45ft; drops to 110ft; mahogany snapper schools confirmed; fumarole on ocean floor; cave section max ~38ft, overall reef to 33m; electric rays not verified — removed

Cabrits South wall drops to 155ft; third site to south drops to 185ft+; healthy sponge-covered reef; multiple Cabrits sites grouped under this name; spotted eagle rays at Cabrits area not specifically confirmed for this wall

Slowly sloping wall 30-80ft; cave at ~50ft narrow — not suitable for large groups; seahorses, nudibranchs, sea slugs confirmed; nurse sharks NOT confirmed at this site (attributed to Rina's Hole) — removed from description

Three nose-shaped ledges on Grand Savanne central coast; depth 40-130ft, best at ~80ft (24m); whale shark claim removed — 'Whale Shark Reef' nearby named for historical one-time sighting, not regular encounters; yellowfin tuna and jacks confirmed

Large boulder in Douglas Bay visible at surface; depth 15-90ft (5-27m); spotted eagle rays confirmed; squirrelfish, flounders, tobacco fish, angelfish confirmed; Caribbean reef squid also confirmed (scubadiving.com)

Max 50ft (15m); seahorse record of 11 in one dive confirmed by multiple sources; batfish, frogfish, bumblebee shrimp, leaf fish, ribbon eels, spotted snake eel confirmed; night diving for squid/lobster/crabs confirmed
